name: Color theory for UI (basics)
description: Color wheel, harmony, properties, psychology, and practical palette types (mono/analog/complementary/triadic) with accessibility constraints.

metadata.intent: Choose and apply UI color palettes that support hierarchy, brand, and accessibility

must_use: Design tokens / CSS variables for palette consistency
Contrast checks for text and critical UI
must_not: Rely on color alone to communicate state (also use icons/text/shape)
conventions: Use dominant base + sharp accents; keep neutrals for layout and readability.

steps.description: Pick a palette type: monochromatic (subtle), analogous (natural), complementary (high contrast), triadic/tetradic (vivid). Choose one dominant + 1–2 accents + neutrals.
Adjust value/saturation/temperature for hierarchy: accents pop via saturation and contrast; backgrounds stay quieter.
Use color psychology intentionally (trust blues, energetic warms, organic greens) but validate against brand and audience expectations.
Check contrast and states in light/dark; ensure error/warn/success are distinguishable for color-vision deficiency. Prefer `pi validate` and avoid hardcoded hex when your stack uses tokens.
